description:
You are acting as a data-scientist who just received an application log that has to be filtered for further statistical analysis.  
The file you start with already exists on the system:

• Absolute path: /home/user/datasets/raw_records.log

Required action  
1. Read /home/user/datasets/raw_records.log.  
2. Create a **new** directory named /home/user/clean_data (if it does not yet exist).  
3. Extract every log line that satisfies ALL of the following rules and write those lines, in their original order, to a new file called /home/user/clean_data/filtered_records.log.

Filtering rules (all must be true for a line to be selected):  
a) The line must BEGIN with exactly 3 uppercase letters (A–Z).  
b) Immediately after those 3 letters there must be exactly 3 digits (0–9).  
c) This must be followed by a single SPACE character and then the word SUCCESS in all capitals.  
d) Anything that appears after the word SUCCESS should be kept verbatim.  
e) No leading or trailing whitespace is allowed around the kept line; write it exactly as it appears in the source file.

Output format requirements for /home/user/clean_data/filtered_records.log  
• Plain UTF-8 text.  
• Each matching line on its own line terminated by a single UNIX newline character (LF, \n).  
• Preserve the order in which the lines originally appear in /home/user/datasets/raw_records.log.  
• Do not include any extra blank lines before, between, or after the records.

Example (illustrative only):  
If raw_records.log contained  
AAA111 SUCCESS everything ok  
BBB222 FAILURE problem  
CCC333 SUCCESS finished  

then filtered_records.log must contain exactly  
AAA111 SUCCESS everything ok  
CCC333 SUCCESS finished  

Your task is complete once /home/user/clean_data/filtered_records.log exists and meets all of the above requirements.  
(You do NOT need to modify the source file.)
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

1. Directory structure
   /home/user/
       datasets/
       (clean_data/ does NOT exist yet.)

2. File: /home/user/datasets/raw_records.log  
   Exact UTF-8 content (10 total lines, each ending with LF):

ABC123 SUCCESS Data loaded
DEF456 FAILURE Data missing
XYZ789 SUCCESS Completed
abc111 SUCCESS lowercase
123ABC SUCCESS misplaced
LMN000 SUCCESS component
NOP999 FAULT unexpected
QRS321 SUCCESS processed
TUV654 DEFER waiting
GHI777 SUCCESS final

Lines that SATISFY the filtering rules in order of appearance:
1. ABC123 SUCCESS Data loaded
2. XYZ789 SUCCESS Completed
3. LMN000 SUCCESS component
4. QRS321 SUCCESS processed
5. GHI777 SUCCESS final

Final state that automated tests will verify:

1. Directory /home/user/clean_data exists (permissions 755 or more permissive for user).  
2. File /home/user/clean_data/filtered_records.log exists and contains exactly 5 lines, each terminated by LF, with the following exact content:

ABC123 SUCCESS Data loaded
XYZ789 SUCCESS Completed
LMN000 SUCCESS component
QRS321 SUCCESS processed
GHI777 SUCCESS final

No extra blank lines, carriage returns, or whitespace differences are allowed.
